turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <!-- Background -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="512" height="512" fill="#ffffff"/>

  <!-- Tail (lightning bolt) behind body -->
  <path d="M330 395 L420 305 L368 305 L402 235 L300 320 L356 320 Z" fill="#FFEB3B" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="6" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
  <path d="M330 395 L420 305 L368 305 L402 235" fill="none" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="6" stroke-linejoin="round"/>

  <!-- Body -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="340" rx="120" ry="145" fill="#FFD93B" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="8"/>

  <!-- Belly patch -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="360" rx="70" ry="95" fill="#FFF3B0" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="6"/>

  <!-- Arms -->
  <path d="M165 330 C130 340, 125 390, 165 405 C195 416, 210 380, 195 355 C185 340, 178 326, 165 330 Z" fill="#FFD93B" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="7"/>
  <path d="M347 330 C382 340, 387 390, 347 405 C317 416, 302 380, 317 355 C327 340, 334 326, 347 330 Z" fill="#FFD93B" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="7"/>

  <!-- Head -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="195" r="135" fill="#FFD93B" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="8"/>

  <!-- Ears -->
  <path d="M155 125 L95 45 L75 155 Z" fill="#FFD93B" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="8" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
  <path d="M357 125 L417 45 L437 155 Z" fill="#FFD93B" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="8" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
  <path d="M145 120 L105 60 L92 135 Z" fill="#F5D000"/>
  <path d="M367 120 L407 60 L420 135 Z" fill="#F5D000"/>

  <!-- Cheeks -->
  <circle cx="195" cy="245" r="22" fill="#FF4D4D"/>
  <circle cx="317" cy="245" r="22" fill="#FF4D4D"/>

  <!-- Eyes -->
  <circle cx="215" cy="205" r="16" fill="#1A1A1A"/>
  <circle cx="297" cy="205" r="16" fill="#1A1A1A"/>
  <circle cx="210" cy="200" r="5" fill="#ffffff"/>
  <circle cx="292" cy="200" r="5" fill="#ffffff"/>

  <!-- Brows / head markings -->
  <path d="M185 185 C205 165, 225 165, 245 185" fill="none" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="8" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <path d="M267 185 C287 165, 307 165, 327 185" fill="none" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="8" stroke-linecap="round"/>

  <!-- Nose & mouth -->
  <path d="M252 225 L260 225 L256 238 Z" fill="#1A1A1A"/>
  <path d="M235 255 C245 270, 267 270, 277 255" fill="none" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>

  <!-- Feet -->
  <path d="M175 470 C175 430, 240 430, 240 470 C240 490, 175 490, 175 470 Z" fill="#FFD93B" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="7"/>
  <path d="M272 470 C272 430, 337 430, 337 470 C337 490, 272 490, 272 470 Z" fill="#FFD93B" stroke="#F2C100" stroke-width="7"/>

  <!-- Small highlight on body -->
  <path d="M212 300 C190 335, 195 385, 225 420" fill="none" stroke="#FFF3B0" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.7"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating Pikachu SVG**

I need to draw Pikachu without any letters, focusing on a simple, stylized version of the electric mouse. The design will include a circular yellow head, triangular ears with dark yellow interiors, and red circles for cheeks. The tail should be shaped like a lightning bolt. I'll keep the background white and ensure the dimensions are 512x512. Other details like small arcs for the mouth and simple arms will complete the look. Lastly, I'll return the design in JSON format.

I'll keep the background white and ensure the dimensions are 512x512. Other details like small arcs for the mouth and simple arms will complete the look. Lastly, I'll return the design in JSON format.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

message.reasoning:
**Generating unique guesses**

I'm analyzing a cute yellow creature that looks like it could be Pikachu from Pokémon. I've got to come up with 20 fitting one-word guesses. So far, I’ve included Pikachu, Raichu, Pokémon, electric, and several others like "mouse" and "rodent" since it's mouse-like. I want to make sure they’re all plausible, so I list them: Pikachu, Raichu, Pokémon, electric, thunder, lightning, volt, sparky, mouse, rodent, chibi, cute, sprite, monster, beast, faerie, spark, jolteon, and electivire. That's my final list!**Refining unique guesses**

I’m focusing on generating a final list of unique one-word guesses. I’ve settled on Pikachu, Jolteon, Raichu, Electabuzz, and Electivire as my top choices. Then I’ll include Pokémon, electric, lightning, thunder, bolt, volt, sparky, spark, rodent, mouse, chibi, cute, monster, sprite, and Thundercat, which is technically one word but has two parts. I should finalize this list and present it clearly. Got it!
